tex:
80 students each record the name of their mathematics teacher.
\newline
The number of these students taught by Mr House and by Miss Patel are shown in the bar chart.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) How many more students are taught by Miss Patel than by Mr House?

(b) 15 students are taught by Mr Smith.
\newline
      Twice as many students are taught by Mrs Brown than by Mr Jones.
\newline

      Use this information to complete the bar chart.
\newline

(c) Write down the mode.
\newline

(d) One of these students is chosen at random.
\newline

      Work out the probability that this student

      (i) (e) is taught by Mr House,

      

(f) is not taught by either Mr House or Miss Patel.
\newline

      

(g) This information is also to be shown in a pie chart.
\newline

      Work out the sector angle for Miss Patel.
\newline

tex:
Mr Lester has a fruit and vegetable shop.
\newline

(a) Apples cost 32 cents each.
\newline
      Suki buys 6 apples.
\newline

      Work out the change Mr Lester gives Suki when she pays with a \$10 note.
\newline

\$ 

(b) Green grapes cost \$3.10 per kilogram.
\newline
      Red grapes cost \$2.80 per kilogram.
\newline

      Work out the total cost of buying 0.6 kg of green grapes and 34 kg of red grapes.
\newline

\$ 

(c) George spends \$12 on fruit each week.
\newline
      The total amount he spends on food is \$75.
\newline

      Work out the percentage of the \$75 he spends on fruit.
\newline

 \%

(d) Mr Lester buys pineapples for \$1.50 each.
\newline
      He makes 60\% profit when he sells them.
\newline

      Work out the selling price of a pineapple.
\newline

\$ 

(e) The table shows the number of bananas bought by the last 50 customers.
\newline

      \begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
      \hline
      Number of bananas bought & Frequency \\
      \hline
      0 & 14 \\
      1 & 0 \\
      2 & 2 \\
      3 & 5 \\
      4 & 11 \\
      5 & 8 \\
      6 & 10 \\
      \hline
      \end{tabular}

      (i) (f) Find the range.
\newline

      

(g) Work out the median.
\newline

      

(h) Calculate the mean.
\newline

stem.content:
Mr Lester has a fruit and vegetable shop.
\newline

_rawBody:
Mr Lester has a fruit and vegetable shop.

\begin{parts}
\item Apples cost 32 cents each.
      Suki buys 6 apples.

      Work out the change Mr Lester gives Suki when she pays with a \$10 note.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item Green grapes cost \$3.10 per kilogram.
      Red grapes cost \$2.80 per kilogram.

      Work out the total cost of buying 0.6 kg of green grapes and 34 kg of red grapes.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}

\item George spends \$12 on fruit each week.
      The total amount he spends on food is \$75.

      Work out the percentage of the \$75 he spends on fruit.

\AnswerLine[] \%
\Marks{1}

\item Mr Lester buys pineapples for \$1.50 each.
      He makes 60\% profit when he sells them.

      Work out the selling price of a pineapple.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item The table shows the number of bananas bought by the last 50 customers.

      \begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
      \hline
      Number of bananas bought & Frequency \\
      \hline
      0 & 14 \\
      1 & 0 \\
      2 & 2 \\
      3 & 5 \\
      4 & 11 \\
      5 & 8 \\
      6 & 10 \\
      \hline
      \end{tabular}

      \begin{subparts}
      \item Find the range.

      \AnswerLine[][]
      \Marks{1}

      \item Work out the median.

      \AnswerLine[][]
      \Marks{1}

      \item Calculate the mean.

      \AnswerLine[][]
      \Marks{3}
      \end{subparts}
\end{parts}

tex:
(a) Calculate the area of a triangle with base 12 cm and height 8 cm.
\newline

 cm$^2$

(b) A trapezium has parallel sides of length 5 cm and 9 cm.
\newline
      The distance between the parallel sides is 6 cm.
\newline

      Calculate the area of the trapezium.
\newline

 cm$^2$

(c) The diagram shows a prism.
\newline

      The cross-section is a trapezium with parallel sides of length 4 cm and 10 cm.
\newline
      The distance between the parallel sides is 3 cm.
\newline
      The length of the prism is 12 cm.
\newline

      Calculate the volume of the prism.
\newline

 cm$^3$

(d) A cylinder has radius 5 cm and height 12 cm.
\newline

      Calculate the volume of the cylinder.
\newline

      Give your answer as a multiple of $\pi$.
\newline

 cm$^3$

content.content:
Calculate the area of a triangle with base 12 cm and height 8 cm.
\newline

A trapezium has parallel sides of length 5 cm and 9 cm.
\newline
      The distance between the parallel sides is 6 cm.
\newline

      Calculate the area of the trapezium.
\newline

The diagram shows a prism.
\newline

      The cross-section is a trapezium with parallel sides of length 4 cm and 10 cm.
\newline
      The distance between the parallel sides is 3 cm.
\newline
      The length of the prism is 12 cm.
\newline

      Calculate the volume of the prism.
\newline

A cylinder has radius 5 cm and height 12 cm.
\newline

      Calculate the volume of the cylinder.
\newline

      Give your answer as a multiple of $\pi$.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item Calculate the area of a triangle with base 12 cm and height 8 cm.

\AnswerLine[] cm$^2$
\Marks{2}

\item A trapezium has parallel sides of length 5 cm and 9 cm.
      The distance between the parallel sides is 6 cm.

      Calculate the area of the trapezium.

\AnswerLine[] cm$^2$
\Marks{2}

\item The diagram shows a prism.

      The cross-section is a trapezium with parallel sides of length 4 cm and 10 cm.
      The distance between the parallel sides is 3 cm.
      The length of the prism is 12 cm.

      Calculate the volume of the prism.

\AnswerLine[] cm$^3$
\Marks{3}

\item A cylinder has radius 5 cm and height 12 cm.

      Calculate the volume of the cylinder.

      Give your answer as a multiple of $\pi$.

\AnswerLine[] cm$^3$
\Marks{3}
\end{parts}

qtype: limits of accuracy, ratio problem, currency conversion, percentage calculation
tex:
Zach goes on holiday.
\newline

(a) The mass, m kilograms, of his suitcase is 23.5 kg, correct to the nearest 500 g.
\newline

      Complete this statement about the value of m.
\newline

 kg $\leq$ m $<$  kg

(b) The ratio of the costs flights : hotels = 3 : 8.
\newline
      The cost of the flights is \$861.
\newline

      Work out the total cost of flights and hotels.
\newline

\$ 

(c) \$1 = 0.88 euros

      £1 = 1.15 euros

      Zach changes \$575 into euros.
\newline
      He spends 45\% of the euros in France.
\newline
      He changes the euros he does not use into pounds (£) to spend in England.
\newline

      Work out how many pounds he receives.
\newline

£

\$1 = 0.88 euros

£ \AnswerLine[]

_rawBody:
Zach goes on holiday.

\begin{parts}
\item The mass, m kilograms, of his suitcase is 23.5 kg, correct to the nearest 500 g.

      Complete this statement about the value of m.

\AnswerLine[] kg $\leq$ m $<$ \AnswerLine[] kg
\Marks{2}

\item The ratio of the costs flights : hotels = 3 : 8.
      The cost of the flights is \$861.

      Work out the total cost of flights and hotels.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item \$1 = 0.88 euros

      £1 = 1.15 euros

      Zach changes \$575 into euros.
      He spends 45\% of the euros in France.
      He changes the euros he does not use into pounds (£) to spend in England.

      Work out how many pounds he receives.

£ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{4}
\end{parts}
